title: glechoma biondiana
intro: Glechoma biondiana, also known as creeping thyme or woodland mint, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North America, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, making it effective against various infections and conditions. The health benefits of Glechoma biondiana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, or applied topically as a poultice to relieve skin irritations and wounds.
